channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The Tragedy of Peter Edward Rose
item.description: Pete Rose, in many ways, bookended my life and career – he was my first hero as a kid, and I saw him break Ty Cobb's hit record. Now, he's gone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers to start Jack Flaherty in Game 1 of NLDS, with Yoshinobu Yamamoto set for Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jack Flaherty will start Game 1 of this weekend’s National League Division Series for the Los Angeles Dodgers, with rookie Yoshinobu Yamamoto set for Game 2.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Days after being suspended Emi Martinez has Prince William celebrating in the Champions League
item.description: Days after being suspended by FIFA, Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez produced heroics in front of the watching Prince William as Aston Villa sealed another famous win against Bayern Munich.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ibáñez's big hit lifts Tigers to 5-2 win over Astros and sweep in AL Wild Card Series
item.description: Andy Ibáñez hit a tiebreaking three-run double in Detroit’s four-run eighth inning, and the Tigers swept the Houston Astros with a 5-2 victory in Game 2 of their AL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dream fire Tanisha Wright in WNBA's 3rd coach firing since playoffs began
item.description: The Atlanta Dream have fired coach Tanisha Wright after three seasons, the franchise announced Wednesday. Wright took over the team in 2022 and helped them reach the playoffs the last two seasons. The Dream lost both years in the opening round of the postseason, including to New York last month.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UFC legend Michael Bisping says he chased down thief who stole wife's bag in Paris
item.description: UFC legend Michael Bisping revealed on Tuesday he had to "stop a crime" while he was in Paris with his wife. Someone stole his wife's bag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knicks add Karl-Anthony Towns from Timberwolves after completing three-team trade with Hornets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Karl-Anthony Towns joined the Knicks on Wednesday after the Minnesota Timberwolves sent their four-time All-Star to New York in a three-team trade that also included the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Newsom extends ‘last call’ until 4 a.m., but only for private club at one sports arena
item.description: Supporters say the bill is designed to benefit the local economy, but opponents fear it could spread up and down the state and jeopardize public safety.
item.dc:creator: Jason Henry, The Mercury News

item.title: Americans get biggest road win and capture the Presidents Cup for the 10th straight time
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — U.S. captain Jim Furyk raised the gold trophy before a team that dressed in red shirts that filled the scoreboard early with red scores. The Presidents Cup is one trophy the Americans own, for two decades and counting.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jalen Hurts and Eagles head to Tampa Bay looking to avenge lopsided postseason loss to Buccaneers
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers know how it feels to go on the road with added motivation to beat the team that eliminated them from the previous season's playoffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White Sox beat playoff-bound Tigers 4-0, stay at 121 losses going into finale
item.description: Sean Burke pitched two-hit ball over five innings, Andrew Benintendi tied his career high with his 20th homer and the Chicago White Sox beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 4-0 on Saturday to stay at 121 losses going into their season finale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SJSU opponent forfeits volleyball match, lawsuit alleges player is transgender
item.description: (KRON) -- Boise State University's volleyball team forfeited a match against San Jose State, the school announced on Friday. The cancellation came after one San Jose State player said in a lawsuit that one of her teammates is transgender.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Jazz management excited about youth movement
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back in April, Jazz CEO of Basketball Operations Danny Ainge had his sights on acquiring a big-name player. "Our objective is to find a player or two, and we're we're ready to roll," Ainge said five months ago. "We're ready to go big game hunting.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Shohei Ohtani heads to 1st postseason after Dodgers clinch NL West title with a 7-2 win over Padres
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is headed to the postseason for the first time in his career after the Los Angeles Dodgers won the NL West on Thursday night by scoring five runs in the seventh inning in a 7-2 win over the second-place San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Derrick Rose, Chicago native and NBA MVP with the Bulls, announces retirement from pro basketball after 16 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Derrick Rose, a Chicago native and city basketball legend in every sense of the word, is retiring from professional basketball. Rose, who grew up in Englewood on the city's Southwest Side and was a prep star at Simeon before being drafted No.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland A's play last game at Coliseum Thursday afternoon
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- After 57 years of Major League Baseball, the Oakland A's will play their last game at the Oakland Coliseum on Thursday afternoon. They will take on the Texas Rangers at 12:37 p.m. The A's are headed to Sacramento next season, and they plan to eventually go to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4
